From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1972 Italian comedy film

| Boccaccio | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Directed by | [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") |
| Written by | [Mario Amendola](/wiki/Mario_Amendola "Mario Amendola") Bruno Corbucci |
| Produced by | [Dino De Laurentiis](/wiki/Dino_De_Laurentiis "Dino De Laurentiis") |
| Cinematography | [Luigi Kuveiller](/wiki/Luigi_Kuveiller "Luigi Kuveiller") |
| Edited by | [Eugenio Alabiso](/wiki/Eugenio_Alabiso "Eugenio Alabiso") |
| Music by | [Carlo Rustichelli](/wiki/Carlo_Rustichelli "Carlo Rustichelli") |
| Production company | Dino de Laurentiis Cinematografica |
| Release date | * 1972 (1972) |
| Running time | 98 minutes |
| Country | [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy") |
| Language | Italian |

***Boccaccio*** (also known as *The Nights of Boccaccio*) is a 1972 Italian [comedy film](/wiki/Comedy_film "Comedy film") written and directed by [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci"). It is loosely based on the [Giovanni Boccaccio](/wiki/Giovanni_Boccaccio "Giovanni Boccaccio")'s novel *[Decameron](/wiki/Decameron "Decameron")*, and it is part of a series of derivative comedies based on the success of [Pier Paolo Pasolini](/wiki/Pier_Paolo_Pasolini "Pier Paolo Pasolini")'s *[The Decameron](/wiki/The_Decameron_(1971_film) "The Decameron (1971 film)")* (1971).[[1]](#cite_note-70dl-1)[[2]](#cite_note-stracult-2)
